text:
Market today: Chip and tech names are in full rally mode - the headline that Micron, Intel, and AMD added $2 trillion in combined value in Q2 tells you exactly what's driving the tape. Your sector is the market right now. Momentum is real but so is the risk of a crowded, extended move.

Your holdings: Everything is green and no SELL/TRIM flags - solid. That said, SOXL (+4.8%) is a 3x leveraged chip ETF - it decays over time and a sudden reversal hits you three times as hard on margin. SPAL is brand-new, very volatile, and moves fast - tiny, but watch it. QQQI (+0.67%) is barely moving; confirm it's still doing its income job.

Watch: Whether the chip rally has follow-through after that massive Q2 run-up - euphoria can flip fast. Also watch Fed signals after the Lisa Cook Supreme Court ruling - any rate surprise reshapes your whole book.

Bottom line: You're well-positioned in the right sector, but this is exactly when margin discipline matters most - don't let winners make you sloppy with stops.
plays: SOXL: Your stop is at $200 vs. current $266 - that's a 25% gap. On margin with a 3x ETF after a massive chip rally, consider tightening your stop meaningfully above $200 to protect gains.
SPAL: Brand-new leveraged ETF, still small, stop at $21.38 vs. $25.63. Keep size small; confirm your stop is live given how fast these move.
No new buys today - scanner setups exist in ALAB and NVDA but the chip space just ran $2T in Q2; chasing here on margin adds real risk.
